# HEADLINE: UN Chief Urges Extension of Fragile US-Iran Ceasefire as Negotiations Continue | YES | HIGH confidence

A **two-week US-Iran ceasefire** was announced on April 8, 2026, following nearly 40 days of intense hostilities[1]. The agreement, mediated by Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and military chief Asim Munir with participation from Türkiye and Egypt, includes the immediate ces

signals.headline: **Nuclear negotiations stalled**: Direct talks between the US and Iran are progressing "somewhat slowly," with Trump ind
signals.raw: **Nuclear negotiations stalled**: Direct talks between the US and Iran are progressing "somewhat slowly," with Trump indicating new negotiations likely in Pakistan within two days (as of April 13)[3]. Trump has rejected any arrangement limiting nuclear enrichment restrictions to a fixed period like 20 years[3].
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by A

signals.headline: **Ceasefire fragility**: While the UN Secretary-General welcomed the move, reports show continued Israeli strikes and ma
signals.raw: **Ceasefire fragility**: While the UN Secretary-General welcomed the move, reports show continued Israeli strikes and mass casualties in Lebanon, with Israeli PM Netanyahu explicitly stating the ceasefire does not include Lebanon[4].

signals.headline: **Economic leverage**: The US maintains a blockade on Iranian ports, effectively halting Iran's business operations, whi
signals.raw: **Economic leverage**: The US maintains a blockade on Iranian ports, effectively halting Iran's business operations, while Iran's military capabilities have been severely degraded[3].
matched_markets: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by A

signals.headline: **Uncertainty on extension**: As of April 14, the UN chief urged the US and Iran to continue the ceasefire and extend it
signals.raw:
**Uncertainty on extension**: As of April 14, the UN chief urged the US and Iran to continue the ceasefire and extend it as needed, with warnings that "there is no military solution"[2]. Trump refused to comment on what happens if no agreement is reached by the ceasefire's end, stating only that "it won't be pleasant for them"[3].
